programmes.show_description: Elton John plays 18 hits spanning his momentous career on Yamaha's incredible Million-dollar piano. Spectacular show from the Colosseum at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas.
Arguably the greatest ever frontman, Freddie Mercury was a complete one-off. This is the Queen icon's story - told through the sale of his personal items at Sotheby's.
In a sale at Bonham's, there are swords from the English Civil War and letters revealing the extent of the love affair between David Ormsby-Gore and Jackie Kennedy. (S9, ep 6)
In London, Christie's auction Francesco Guardi's Rialto Bridge painting, while Sotheby's are selling Turner's view of the fortress at Ehrenbreitstein, valued at £15million. (S9, ep 7)
Somewhere: Musician Nwando Ebizie and director Juliet Ellis connect as dancer Jahmiah Simpson floats between the waking world and the dream world. (3 of 3)

programmes.show_name: Classic FM Live: Great British Classics
programmes.show_description: A celebration of great British classics.
William and Mary: When her husband William dies, Mary Pearl is shocked that his will stipulates that his brain should be kept alive - with interesting results. (S1, ep 3)
Lamb to the Slaughter: When Mary kills her husband with a frozen leg of lamb, she must think quickly to hide the evidence. (S1, ep 4)
The Landlady: When a young man arrives in Bath, he is shocked to find his seemingly charming landlady has a dark side. (S1, ep 5)
Neck: A young lady is cut down to size by her husband's loyal butler when it is discovered she is having an affair. (S1, ep 6)
Edward the Conqueror: When Edward grows tired of his wife Louisa's obsession with a stray cat and decides to get rid of it, Louisa exacts her revenge. (S1, ep 7)
A Dip in the Pool: An American gambler must come up with a cunning plan to slow down a ship at sea in order to win a bet. (S1, ep 8)
Anyone For Murder?: A psychology professor places an ad offering to help spouses get rid of their other halves, grabbing the police's attention. Trouble ensues. (S2, ep 20)
Beast in View: An attorney helps a client threatened by an unstable woman who blames her for a broken wedding engagement. (S2, ep 21)

programmes.show_name: Michael Flatley Lord Of The Dance: Dangerous Games
programmes.show_description: Choreographed by Michael Flatley, 40 of the world's most talented performers combine the best of traditional and contemporary music and dance.
Rare performances, interviews and rarely seen footage come together to paint a picture of the history and origins of Motown.
Best Of: Stephen Mangan presents this special episode featuring highlights from the latest series of Landscape Artist of the Year. (S11, ep 10)
...& The Crickets: The definitive story of how Buddy Holly became an icon of rock's first generation. Features the hits That'll Be The Day and Peggy Sue. Narrated by Paul Gambaccini.
Offering extraordinary insight, Roy Orbison deep dives into what made his music a cultural driving force through a series of interviews collected over several years.
A wandering gunfighter plays two rival families against each other in a town torn apart by greed, pride and revenge. (1964)
Explore the life of the highly influential director credited as the inventor of the Italian Western genre. His films include Once Upon a Time In America. (S3, ep 10)

programmes.show_name: Pink Floyd: A Delicate Sound of Thunder
programmes.show_description: Take in a spectacular concert film from Pink Floyd's 1988 'A Momentary Lapse of Reason Tour', featuring hits like Wish You Were Here and Time.
A film from long-time Pink Floyd collaborator Ian Emes, offering a captivating and intuitive interpretation of the Rock'n'Roll legends' music.
The line-up changed, the style evolved, but the hits kept coming. From Peter Gabriel to Phil Collins, Invisible Touch to I Can't Dance, a documentary that charts the inspiration of Genesis.
The Joshua Tree: Bono, The Edge, Adam Clayton, Larry Mullen and producers Brian Eno and Daniel Lanois give an insight into the making of their 1987 smash The Joshua Tree. (S1, ep 18)
Aya Eban: Created by NikNak, Randy Duburke and Peta-Gaye Brown, follow a character stuck in a hectic city without peace - until their ancestors help them discover their power. (2 of 3)
Accompanied by the Johann Strauss Orchestra, world renowned violinist Andre Rieu performs numbers including Roses from the South, and Emperor's Waltz at Schonbrunn in Vienna.
The Way Up To Heaven: Mrs Foster exacts an intriguing revenge on her husband after his dawdling causes her to miss a flight. (S1, ep 9)
Depart in Peace: Art dealer Lionel gets an artist to visualise his fiancee Janet nude and paint her accordingly. Janet, however, does not see the funny side. (S1, ep 10)
My Ladylove, My Dove: When Pamela and Arthur invite a young couple over for the weekend, things take a surprising turn. (S1, ep 11)
Taste: A dinner party takes a surprising turn when one pretentious guest is challenged to a wine-tasting contest. (S1, ep 12)
Behind the Locked Door: A young couple tries to convince the bride's mother that their love is sincere. (S2, ep 22)
Best Of: Stephen Mangan presents this special episode featuring highlights from the latest series of Landscape Artist of the Year. (S11, ep 10)
Sky Original documentary tracing the complex history of Michelangelo's most controversial commission, the Mausoleum of Pope Julius II, which took 40 years to complete.
A look at the life and career of two-time Academy Award-winning British film icon Sir Michael Caine. (S10, ep 4)
...Remembering The Carpenters. Exploration of the highs and lows of the lives and careers of the brother-sister duo, including Karen's untimely death in 1983. With Burt Bacharach.

programmes.show_name: Luther Vandross: Live At The Royal Albert Hall
programmes.show_description: Enjoy this glitzy, upbeat celebration of the greatest music from Luther Vandross, featuring a full gospel choir.

programmes.show_name: Rod Stewart: It Had To Be You
programmes.show_description: Filmed in front of a live studio audience at Sony Studios in New York City, Rod Stewart performs songs from his recent release "It Had to be You: The Great American Songbook".
A historical drama film about the founding of the State of Israel. Contains outdated attitudes and cultural depictions which viewers may find inconsistent with contemporary values.
